title: Subscription Model
shortDefinition: Charge recurring fees (monthly or yearly) for ongoing access to a product or service.

content.definition: A subscription model charges customers a recurring fee at regular intervals (typically monthly or annually) for continuous access to a product or service. Revenue is predictable and recurring, unlike one-time purchases. Customers either renew or churn each period, making retention the core lever.
content.whyItMatters: Subscriptions create predictable, recurring revenue (MRR/ARR), which is invaluable for raising capital and planning growth. Instead of selling one product once, you sell an annuity. But it's also unforgiving: churn compounds. A 5% monthly churn kills 50% of your customers in 12 months. You must obsess over retention, not just acquisition. Most SaaS companies die from poor unit economics, not low growth.
content.howToApply: Start with unit economics: CAC (customer acquisition cost) should be recovered within 12 months. If CAC is $1,000 and ARPU is $50/month, your payback is 20 months—too long. Build a cohort retention curve and track MRR, NRR (net revenue retention—if you have upsells), and churn. The formula for sustainable growth: (New MRR - Churned MRR) / Previous MRR = MoM growth rate. If it's under 5%, you're not growing fast enough relative to churn. Price in tiers (Starter, Pro, Enterprise) to capture different customer segments; your ability to upsell pays for acquisition costs.
commonMistakes: Chasing growth without fixing churn: a 200% growth rate with 40% annual churn is not a business—it's a leak. Fix churn before growth.

Setting price too low: if $19/month feels cheap to you, it's too cheap. 80% of customers would pay more than you're charging. Raise prices 10-20% and watch MRR; if NRR drops below 95%, you've raised too much.

Ignoring expansion revenue: upsells (upgrades within existing customers) are 5-10x cheaper than new customer acquisition. Spend half your energy building expansion motions (add-ons, usage-based upsells, seat expansion).
content.ideafuelConnection: IdeaFuel's Business Plan Generator lets you model subscription economics: set pricing tiers, project churn rates, calculate payback periods, and forecast MRR growth. Test different price points and see how churn sensitivity affects profitability.

seoTitle: What is Subscription Model? | IdeaFuel Glossary
seoDescription: Learn subscription pricing for SaaS startups. MRR, churn, CAC payback, and unit economics fundamentals.
